hub_subtitle: Inglês para o trabalho — do aperto de mão ao acordo fechado, em 7 aulas

lesson.menu_desc: Read two real quotes side by side and choose the better offer -- compare the price per ton, the discount, the delivery and the total -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 6 -- Prices, Volumes and Quotes
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ziláudio | Lesson 6 -- Prices, Volumes and Quotes

listenings.text: Hi Zilaudio, this is Mark from Grainline in Chicago. I am calling about the quote I sent you this morning. I want to give you our best price. The price is one thousand nine hundred reais per ton, but for orders over one thousand tons, I can add a discount of two percent. The delivery is free, in fourteen days. And you have thirty days to pay after the invoice. Please compare our quote with the others, and call me back before Friday. Thank you, and talk to you soon.

Good morning, and here is a short reminder about your two quotes. Before you choose, check three things on every quote. First, the price per ton, and remember that the cheapest price is not always the best deal. Second, the delivery: is it free, and how many days? Third, the payment: how much time do you have to pay after the invoice? Look at the total, not only the price per ton. If the delivery is free and the payment time is longer, a higher price can still be the better offer. Have a good week.

paras: Dear Zilaudio, here are the two quotes you asked for. Grainline, in Chicago, offers a price of 1,900 reais per ton. Riverport, in Rotterdam, offers 1,850 reais per ton, so Riverport is cheaper per ton.

But the price per ton is not the whole story. Grainline gives free delivery in 14 days and a 2% discount for orders over 1,000 tons. Riverport charges 200 reais for delivery, it is slower, and it gives no discount.

Look at the total and the payment time, not only the price per ton. For 1,000 tons, Riverport's total is a little lower, but Grainline gives you more time to pay: 30 days, not 15. The cheaper quote is not always the better deal.
reading.source: Two quotes prepared for Ziláudio for the English for Business & Agribusiness package

reading.prompt: What is the best title for these two documents?
choices: A History of Soybean Farming in Brazil
Two Quotes for Zilaudio -- Compare the Price, the Delivery and the Total
How to Drive a Truck from Sorriso to the Port

items: Riverport's price per ton is lower than Grainline's.
"Riverport offers 1,850 reais per ton" -- under Grainline's 1,900.
Riverport gives a discount for orders over 1,000 tons.
"Riverport gives no discount" -- only Grainline gives one.
Grainline's payment time is shorter than Riverport's.
Grainline gives 30 days, Riverport 15 -- Grainline's is longer.

bank.label: How to talk about a quote
items: What is your price per ton?
Grainline gives free delivery and a discount.
Can you give me a discount for a bigger order?
The total is more important than the price per ton.
I need 30 days to pay after the invoice.

items.situation: A supplier calls. You want to know the price for one ton. Ask him.
tips: What is your price per ton?

items.situation: One quote is 1,850 and the other is 1,900 per ton. Compare them.
Your order is big: 1,200 tons. Ask politely for a lower price.
tips: Can you give me a discount for a bigger order?
discount = money off the normal price.

items.situation: The seller asks when you can pay. You need a month after the invoice.
tips: I need 30 days to pay after the invoice.
invoice = the document that asks you to pay.

items.situation: Explain why the cheaper quote is not always the best.
tips: The total is more important than the price per ton.
total = all the numbers added together.

items.situation: You choose Grainline because delivery is free. Say it.
tips: I choose Grainline because the delivery is free.
delivery = bringing the goods to you.
